MH370 Experiment Dossier (MH-001) — Consolidated Final Report
Version: 1.1 (Consolidated) | Date: 2026-01-09
Maintainer: Jacob Moses Bookbinder
Theory: BSAT (Structural Admissibility Under Refinement)
Status: REPLAYABLE CORE (Internal) | INCOMPLETE (External/DB Provenance)
 
I. Executive Summary: The Structural Thesis
The MH-001 experiment successfully moves from narrative-driven inquiry to a witness-stack pipeline. The "Turning Point" thesis—positing that structural dependencies (parents, transitions, and lattices) are more stable than event labels—is supported by a 1.0 recall victory in internal trials.
•	Total Witnesses: 530 (317 States, 213 Transitions).
•	Key Achievement: Integration of an operational lattice that enforces typed observables, preventing the "axis confusion" (e.g., Geometry Z vs. Altitude) that typically plagues flight path analysis.
 
II. Protocol, Gates, and Governance
Admissibility is governed by the Decision Precedence: HALT > INCOMPLETE > PASS. No data enters the core spine without passing the following checks:
1. The Compatibility Predicate
A domain is only admitted if $Compat(domain) = 1$. This requires an ordered state space, a realization map, and shard-indexed measurement maps.
2. Gate G0: Claim Taxonomy
To prevent "interpretive drift," every statement is labeled. Conjectures are never presented as Empirical Facts.
•	Scope Lock: The foundation is non-causal. We do not discovery laws; we audit witnesses.
•	Operator Containment: The $\Phi/\Theta/\Lambda$ vocabulary is strictly a naming layer for operator roles and does not imply external metaphorical meaning.
3. Branch and Quarantine Discipline
•	Branch Preservation: Contradictory latitude branches (N/S) are kept open.
•	Hype Quarantine: Viral or amplification-driven overlays are preserved for audit but gated from driving conclusions.
 
III. Evidence Inventory: The Witness Stack
